source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure that the cluster-admin role is only used where required

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure that the cluster-admin role is only used where required
input_row.description: The RBAC role cluster-admin provides wide-ranging powers over the environment and should be used only where and when needed.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes provides a set of default roles where RBAC is used. Some of these roles such as cluster-admin provide wide-ranging privileges which should only be applied where absolutely necessary. Roles such as cluster-admin allow super-user access to perform any action on any resource. When used in a ClusterRoleBinding, it gives full control over every resource in the cluster and in all namespaces. When used in a RoleBinding, it gives full control over every resource in the rolebinding's namespace, including the namespace itself. Impact: Care should be taken before removing any clusterrolebindings from the environment to ensure they were not required for operation of the cluster. Specifically, modifications should not be made to clusterrolebindings with the system: prefix as they are required for the operation of system components.
input_row.audit: Obtain a list of the principals who have access to the cluster-admin role by reviewing the clusterrolebinding output for each role binding that has access to the cluster- admin role. kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o=custom- columns=NAME:.metadata.name,ROLE:.roleRef.name,SUBJECT:.subjects[*].name Review each principal listed and ensure that cluster-admin privilege is required for it.
input_row.remediation: Identify all clusterrolebindings to the cluster-admin role. Check if they are used and if they need this role or if they could use a role with fewer privileges. Where possible, first bind users to a lower-privileged role and then remove the clusterrolebinding to the cluster-admin role : kubectl delete clusterrolebinding [name] Default Value: By default a single clusterrolebinding called cluster-admin is provided with the system:masters group as its principal. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/
input_row.impact: Care should be taken before removing any clusterrolebindings from the environment to ensure they were not required for operation of the cluster. Specifically, modifications should not be made to clusterrolebindings with the system: prefix as they are required for the operation of system components.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control involves auditing the use of the `cluster-admin` role, which is part of Kubernetes Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) systems. The described method for auditing involves querying Kubernetes for cluster role bindings and checking which principals are assigned to the `cluster-admin` role. This is a standard Kubernetes RBAC access control property and can be accessed using Kubernetes commands through GCP services.

The `kubectl` command is used to retrieve role bindings, which is part of standard Kubernetes operations that can be automated. Furthermore, these can be executed programmatically by integrating with Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) using the Google Cloud SDK.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_admin_role_audit

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP API/SDK:** Use the Kubernetes API in conjunction with Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) and Google Cloud SDK.
- **Command to Execute:** Utilize `kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o=custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,ROLE:.roleRef.name,SUBJECT:.subjects[*].name` to fetch cluster role bindings data.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Cluster Role Bindings within a GCP Kubernetes Cluster.
- **Configuration Properties to Check:** Identify bindings to the `cluster-admin` role and enumerate associated principals.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** No unnecessary principals have the `cluster-admin` role.
  - **Fail:** Any non-essential principals have the `cluster-admin` role.
- **Expected Values:** Principals assigned to the `cluster-admin` role must be justified and necessary for cluster operations.

This automated check leverages available Kubernetes commands and can be integrated into a continuous security posture management process to ensure that high-privilege roles are only used where necessary.
